title: Discussing poetry
setting: At a cultural evening, conversation turns to Rubén Darío. The skill: an elevated, literary register and genuine appreciation.

steps.speaker: Profesora
steps.es: ¿Ha leído usted a Darío? Es nuestro mayor poeta.
steps.en: Have you read Darío? He's our greatest poet.

options.es: Sí, su musicalidad me cautivó por completo.
options.en: Yes, his musicality captivated me completely.
options.feedback: 'Musicalidad' and 'cautivó' — precise literary vocabulary delivered naturally. Elegant.
True, but the register's too casual for a literary discussion. Reach for richer words.
Honest — and an invitation for her to share. But if you have read him, show that appreciation.

steps.speaker: Profesora
steps.en: And what does his poetry evoke for you?

options.es: Una nostalgia hermosa por lo que se va y no vuelve.
options.en: A beautiful nostalgia for what leaves and doesn't return.
options.feedback: Poetic and perceptive — you're discussing literature AS literature. Genuinely C2.

options.es: Me hace sentir cosas, no sé bien cómo decirlo.
options.en: It makes me feel things, I'm not sure how to say it.
options.feedback: Sincere, but at this level you CAN articulate it. Trust your vocabulary and reach.

options.es: Sobre todo, la belleza del idioma mismo.
options.en: Above all, the beauty of the language itself.
options.feedback: A thoughtful, layered answer — appreciating the language as its own art. Sophisticated.

steps.speaker: Profesora
steps.es: Me sorprende su sensibilidad. ¿De dónde es usted?
steps.en: Your sensitivity surprises me. Where are you from?

options.es: De fuera, aunque el español me adoptó hace tiempo.
options.en: From abroad, though Spanish adopted me long ago.
options.feedback: 'El español me adoptó' — a graceful, almost poetic way to put it. Beautiful turn of phrase.

options.es: Soy extranjero, pero amo esta lengua.
options.en: I'm a foreigner, but I love this language.
options.feedback: Sincere and warm, in fitting register. A lovely, honest answer.
Fine, but a touch flat after such a graceful question. Match her register with some warmth.

steps.speaker: Profesora
steps.es: Pues ama nuestra lengua más que muchos nativos.
steps.en: Well, you love our language more than many natives.

options.es: Quien llega de fuera a veces ve la belleza con ojos nuevos.
options.en: One who arrives from outside sometimes sees the beauty with fresh eyes.
options.feedback: Wise and quotable — an almost aphoristic reflection. This is the summit of expression.

options.es: Es un honor escuchar eso de usted.
options.en: It's an honor to hear that from you.
options.feedback: Gracious and appropriately humble in an elevated setting. Well judged.

options.en: Thanks, I do what I can.
options.feedback: Modest, but a bit plain for the eloquence she just praised. Let your range show.
